title: primula calliantha subsp. mishmiensis
intro: Primula calliantha subsp. mishmiensis, also known as the Mishmi primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. This plant is native to the Himalayas and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Mishmi primrose is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and is also valued for its anti-diabetic and antimicrobial properties. The plant's roots and leaves are used in herbal preparations, often made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal benefits.
